task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField application is open, and the layer panel or project title indicates that 'world_survey' is loaded (e.g., showing layers like 'world_capitals' and 'field_observations').

task_completion.description: Locate the distress signal coordinates in the description field of 'Ottawa'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The feature information or attribute panel for the capital city 'Ottawa' is open, and the description field displays the text 'SAR Target: Lat 30.10, Lon 31.50'.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the distress signal coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QField map view is centered on or near Latitude 30.10, Longitude 31.50. The coordinate display (if visible) or map context should confirm this location.

task_completion.description: Add a new point feature to the 'field_observations' layer at the target location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: QField is in digitizing/editing mode with the 'field_observations' layer selected as the active layer, and the crosshair is positioned at the target coordinates.
The attribute entry form for the newly added point feature is open, and 'SAR_Team_Alpha' is entered into the appropriate name field before saving.

integrity.description: Agent used the QField GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the QField app interface (menus, map navigation, attribute forms) rather than a terminal or SQLite database editor.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual QField application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The QField Android app UI is clearly visible, with its characteristic map view, layer panel, and digitizing tools, not a text editor or different GIS app.

integrity.description: Agent did not use scripts to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows, Python scripts, or automated database queries visible in the screenshots used to insert the feature directly into the geopackage.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target coordinates are Latitude 30.10, Longitude 31.50. The clue text located in Ottawa's description is 'SAR Target: Lat 30.10, Lon 31.50'. The new point feature must be named 'SAR_Team_Alpha' and added to the 'field_observations' layer. The tolerance for the location is 0.05 degrees.
